steps.title: Connect songs to results
steps.content: The songs referenced clam gardens, and archaeological evidence showed the described practice corresponded with larger and more abundant clams despite greater harvesting. That means the songs preserved practical ecological knowledge that worked.

steps.title: Avoid overgeneralizing
steps.content: Choice A makes practical ecological knowledge the defining trait of some music, which is too broad. Choice B speculates about why people sang about the gardens. Choice C invents a species difference. The finding supports effective cultivation knowledge in Indigenous songs. The answer is D.
